subject: Geography
title: Polynesian Navigation
description: A quick win: understand Polynesian Navigation.

hook.text: Polynesians settled thousands of Pacific islands using only stars, waves, and bird flight.

buildup.text: They crossed vast open ocean in double-hulled canoes, carrying plants and animals.

discovery.text: By reading wave patterns reflected off distant islands, they could sense land beyond sight.

twist.text: They reached Hawaii, Easter Island, and New Zealand—a triangle spanning 25 million square km.

climax.text: This was the greatest feat of open-ocean navigation before modern instruments.

punchline.text: They mapped the Pacific with no maps at all.

quiz.question: How did Polynesians navigate the open Pacific?
options: By reading stars, waves, and bird flight patterns
By following ocean currents on rafts
